meta.description: Coaching tips for Level 1-2: AI-Assisted Programming Basics

tips.tip: Instead of googling error messages, try pasting them directly into Claude Code and asking what went wrong.
tips.example_after: I'm getting 'TypeError: Cannot read property of undefined' on line 42 of app.js. What's causing this?

tips.tip: When asking Claude a question, mention the file name and what you were trying to do — it helps AI give a more accurate answer.
tips.example_after: In src/auth.js, the login function returns undefined instead of the user object. I was trying to add JWT auth.

tips.tip: If AI's first answer isn't quite right, don't start over — tell it what was wrong and ask it to adjust.
tips.example_after: That fix handles the null case but not the empty string case. Can you also check for empty strings?

tips.tip: Before accepting AI-generated code, read it line by line. Can you explain what each part does?
tips.example_before: [Accepts AI code, runs it, confused when it breaks]
tips.example_after: Let me read through this... line 5 uses a regex I don't understand. Can you explain what /^[a-z]+$/i matches?

tips.tip: After AI generates code, try to think of one edge case it might have missed. Ask AI to handle it.
tips.example_after: What happens if the user submits an empty form? I don't see validation for empty fields.

tech_variants.react: What happens if the component receives null props? I don't see a null check.
tech_variants.python: What happens if the input is None or an empty string? I don't see type checking.
tech_variants.go: What happens if the error is non-nil? I don't see error handling for this case.

tips.tip: It's good practice to modify AI code to match your project's style — variable naming, error handling patterns, etc.
tips.example_after: I'll rename 'data' to 'userProfile' and 'result' to 'authResponse' to match our naming convention.

tips.tip: Try typing a function signature and pausing — let AI suggest the implementation before you write it yourself.
tips.example_before: function validateEmail(email) { // writes entire function manually }
tips.example_after: function validateEmail(email) { // pause, review AI suggestion, accept or modify }

tech_variants.python: def validate_email(email: str) -> bool:  # pause, review AI suggestion
tech_variants.go: func validateEmail(email string) bool {  // pause, review AI suggestion

tips.tip: Write a comment describing what you want, then let AI complete the code. Comments are prompts too!
tips.example_after:
// Sort users by last login date, most recent first
// [AI suggests implementation]
